body.title: [triage] An intention has NO validation field. Add validations[] (evidence/falsifier/horizon) + the PENDING-VALIDATION state — shipping is not proof.
body.intention: I intend that every intention carries the feedback that comes back to it — its validation — with named evidence, a named falsifier, and a horizon, so that no intention can sit un-tested merely by never being contradicted.
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-13: 'every intention should have a feedback that comes back to it. that's the proof. or the learning.' And: 'long running intentions can have long running validations sometimes even multiple ones perhaps... if I come up with the perfect way to implement Igor, we won't know that for months... so when we finish building that's going to fulfill one intention. perhaps when we finish some piece of training that fulfills another one. but the larger one is still ongoing until it's proven as well.'

SHIPPING IS NOT PROOF. Proof-on-close is a TICKET gate and correct at that level. An INTENTION is not proven by the tickets beneath it closing: 'we built Igor the way we said' and 'the way we said was RIGHT' are different claims, and only the first lands with the code. Intentions NEST, and a parent's validation is NOT the conjunction of its children's — every child can be fulfilled and the parent still be wrong.

MEASURED 2026-07-13: the store holds FIVE intentions. NONE has a validation field. NONE names a falsifier. Their proof, where it exists at all, is prose.

WHAT CHANGES:
1. SCHEMA — an intention gains `validations: [ {evidence, falsifier, horizon, status, observations[]} ]`. PLURAL: a long-running intention may carry several, each with its own horizon.
2. STATUS VOCABULARY — add PENDING-VALIDATION. Today the vocabulary is proven | shipped-unproven, and it is a TICKET vocabulary. There is no state for 'built, and the proof is RUNNING, and it takes four months.' Calling that shipped-unproven is WRONG and corrosive: shipped-unproven names a MISSING PROOF-LEVER, and its whole value is that the nuisance stays visible until someone finds the lever. A long-running validation HAS a lever — it is swinging, it just has not landed. Without this state every long-horizon intention is either mislabelled done or slandered unprovable.
3. THE PROMPT — a horizon field is useless unless something READS it. Nothing will ever re-check a months-out validation on its own. devlab/claudecode/consequence_aging.py ALREADY does exactly this shape for consequence tickets (lists gates that have come due while the item is still open, calm signal, never gates day-close). EXTEND IT — do not birth a new tool (homogeneity over special-case; resist artifact proliferation).

THE GUARD (CP1) — an unfalsifiable validation is a BELIEF, not a proof. A months-long horizon with no defined evidence is indistinguishable from a validation that will never happen: 'still ongoing' becomes a permanent parking spot and the intention is never tested, merely never contradicted. So every validation MUST name what would FALSIFY it. Akien's own words (I-compiled-inference-residue): 'a cached answer with no warden on it... the discipline isn't choosing useful beliefs — it's never letting one forget it was chosen.' THE FALSIFIER IS THE WARDEN. Second guard: SURVIVORSHIP — 'it's been going fine for months' is the ABSENCE of a complaint, and per R-feedback-is-unconditional-silence-is-never-success, absence of feedback is NEVER a pass. A long-running validation needs an ACTIVE evidence stream, not a quiet period.

**Affected files:** devlab/runtime/memory/intentions/*.json (5 records — backfill), devlab/claudecode/memory_emit.py (the emit chokepoint; `intentions` category), devlab/claudecode/consequence_aging.py (extend to surface due validations), skills/intend/SKILL.md (the authoring path must ASK for the falsifier), validity_sweep.py (an intention with no falsifier is a flaggable condition).

**Design rules:** homogeneity-over-special-case (extend consequence_aging, no new tool); resist-artifact-proliferation; R-feedback-is-unconditional-silence-is-never-success; proof-on-close (this EXTENDS it to intentions, does not replace it); CP1 (an unfalsifiable claim is a confident nothing).

**Scope boundary:** IN — the validations[] schema; the PENDING-VALIDATION state and its distinction from shipped-unproven; the due-validation prompt (extending consequence_aging); backfilling the 5 existing intentions with at least evidence+falsifier; /intend asking for the falsifier at authoring time. OUT — the NEXUS return-channel / usage-record plumbing (that is the pipeline surface of the same rule — separate ticket); rewriting the ticket-level proof-on-close gate (unchanged and still correct).

**Test plan:** red -> green. RED today: a check over devlab/runtime/memory/intentions/ reports 5 intentions, 0 with a validation, 0 with a falsifier. GREEN: every intention carries >=1 validation with a non-empty falsifier. Fixtures a hollow build could NOT pass: (1) an intention whose validation has evidence but NO falsifier is FLAGGED — this is the one that matters, because a validation without a falsifier is the exact hollow artifact this ticket exists to prevent; (2) a validation past its horizon with zero observations is SURFACED by the aging prompt (proving the horizon is actually read, not merely stored).

**Proof obligation:** the hollow build is: add a `validations: []` key to five JSON files, run a green check, declare done. What a hollow build could NOT pass — fixture (1): the falsifier must be non-empty, so a validation that cannot fail is rejected at the gate. What NO check can discharge, and must be stated honestly rather than faked (CP1): whether the falsifier NAMED is one that could actually fire. That is a judgement, it is Akien's, and it is why /intend must ASK rather than infer.

**Completion criteria:**
  1. `validations[]` is in the intention schema and enforced at the memory_emit chokepoint.
  2. PENDING-VALIDATION exists in the status vocabulary and is documented as DISTINCT from shipped-unproven (with the why: lever-swinging vs lever-missing).
  3. All 5 existing intentions backfilled with >=1 validation carrying evidence AND falsifier AND horizon — authored WITH Akien, not inferred by CC.
  4. consequence_aging.py (EXTENDED, not duplicated) surfaces validations whose horizon has come due while unfulfilled.
  5. /intend asks for the falsifier at authoring time; a missing falsifier is flagged, not silently accepted.
  6. The red run (5 intentions / 0 falsifiers) is recorded before the green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
